yesterdayAnalysis: Solid 1.2mi open water swim at Carlsbad (36 TSS) — excellent race-specific preparation with tomorrow's SDIT 1K swim in mind. Open water exposure this close to race day builds confidence and dialed sighting/navigation skills. Kept intensity controlled, preserving energy for race day.
todayRecommendation: Execute the planned shakeout: 20min easy spin + 10min easy jog, nothing more. This is all about neuromuscular activation and flushing legs, not fitness. Save every ounce of energy for tomorrow's SDIT race rehearsal.
weekProgress: Week 10 of 13: at 211 TSS with 1 day remaining (70% complete). Schedule is appropriately tapered for tomorrow's race — Monday easy spin (no brick), Tuesday 2,500m sharp swim, Wednesday recovery swim, Thursday short brick, Friday complete rest, today shakeout. Perfect race-week execution for SDIT dress rehearsal.
keyInsight: This is a textbook race-week taper — every session has been purpose-built around tomorrow's SDIT performance as a critical Oregon execution rehearsal.
positives: Open water swim exposure 1 day before race — race-ready preparation
